ol.li: Stack Ownership: The Trampoline acts as a protocol bridge, manually decrementing the VM stack_top and populating physical registers to satisfy the System V AMD64 ABI.
Context Persistence: The InterpFrame* is pushed onto the physical stack (rsp) immediately upon entry, as rdi is volatile and overwritten during the marshalling sequence.
Data Integrity: The native result in rax is committed to the VM stack before the VM dispatch loop resumes, ensuring side-effect transparency.
Register Constraints: System V allows 6 GPR arguments. Additional arguments (Arg 7+) would require RSP-relative stack adjustments, omitted in this implementation.

g.title: Preserve InterpFrame* (Passed in RDI) for Result Commit phase
Store RAX native result into VM stack
